source.document: EU Code of Practice for General-Purpose AI Models – Safety and Security Chapter

commitments.title: Safety & Security Framework
commitments.description: Establish, implement, and maintain a comprehensive Safety & Security Framework

criteria.title: Create a Safety & Security Framework
criteria.requirement: Create a state-of-the-art Safety & Security Framework covering systemic risk assessment and mitigation, including trigger points, acceptance tiers, mitigation expectations, timelines, external input, responsibility allocation, and update process.
criteria.assessment_question: Has the provider created a documented safety and security framework that covers the required elements of the Code’s Safety & Security Framework?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Mitigation expectations per tier.
Documentation includes Update process + changelog approach.
Indicative keywords/phrases: create, state-of-the-art, safety, security, framework, covering, systemic, risk, assessment, mitigation, trigger, points

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. The requirement can reasonably be considered defined at a documentation level (i.e., sufficiently specified to guide implementation), with no material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: Some relevant concepts or elements required by the measure are present, but they are incomplete, implicit, vaguely described, not clearly delineated, or only partially mapped to the measure’s expected evidence. As a result, the measure is only partially addressed at a documentation level.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: No meaningful evidence addressing the requirement is present in the provided documentation, or the documentation contradicts the requirement.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: Implement the Safety & Security Framework
criteria.requirement: Implement the Framework continuously across the model lifecycle, including trigger-point evaluations, post-market monitoring, incident inputs, and escalation to full systemic risk assessment.
criteria.assessment_question: Is the Framework implemented operationally across the model lifecycle with trigger points, monitoring, incident intake, and escalation to full assessment?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Lifecycle risk procedures / SOPs.
Documentation includes Post-market monitoring plan + outputs.
Documentation includes Incident intake + triage process.
Indicative keywords/phrases: implement, framework, continuously, across, model, lifecycle, trigger-point, evaluations, post-market, monitoring, incident, inputs

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. The requirement can reasonably be considered defined at a documentation level (i.e., sufficiently specified to guide implementation), with no material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: Some relevant concepts or elements required by the measure are present, but they are incomplete, implicit, vaguely described, not clearly delineated, or only partially mapped to the measure’s expected evidence. As a result, the measure is only partially addressed at a documentation level.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: No meaningful evidence addressing the requirement is present in the provided documentation, or the documentation contradicts the requirement.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: Framework updates
criteria.requirement: Update and reassess the Framework periodically and when conditions indicate, including versioning, changelogs, and remediation plans where needed.
criteria.assessment_question: Are Framework reassessments and updates defined (including periodicity/triggers), versioned, and followed by remediation plans when required?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Trigger criteria for reassessment.
Indicative keywords/phrases: update, reassess, framework, periodically, conditions, indicate, versioning, changelogs, remediation, plans, needed, assessment

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. The requirement can reasonably be considered defined at a documentation level (i.e., sufficiently specified to guide implementation), with no material gaps.

compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: AI Office notification
criteria.requirement: Provide the AI Office with unredacted access to the Framework and updates within five business days.
criteria.assessment_question: Is there evidence of timely AI Office notification/access within 5 business days of confirmation?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Submission receipts/logs with timestamps.
Documentation includes Confirmation date vs submission date mapping.
Indicative keywords/phrases: provide, office, unredacted, access, framework, updates, within, five, business, days, notification, sop

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. commitments.title: Documentation & Transparency
commitments.description: Maintain detailed documentation and provide appropriate public transparency

criteria.title: Additional documentation
criteria.requirement: Maintain additional detailed documentation (architecture, integration, evaluations, mitigations) for the AI Office upon request and retain it for at least 10 years after market placement; track key decisions and justifications.
criteria.assessment_question: Is additional documentation maintained, traceable, and retained for at least 10 years?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: maintain, additional, detailed, documentation, architecture, integration, evaluations, mitigations, office, upon, request, retain

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: Public transparency
criteria.requirement: Publish appropriate public summaries of the Framework and Model Reports when necessary for systemic risk assessment/mitigation, with safeguards for sensitive info and mitigation effectiveness; document exceptions.
criteria.assessment_question: Are public transparency summaries provided when necessary, with safeguards and documented exceptions?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Redaction rationale and approvals.
Indicative keywords/phrases: publish, appropriate, public, summaries, framework, model, reports, necessary, systemic, risk, assessment, mitigation

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. commitments.title: Systemic Risk Identification
commitments.description: Identify and develop scenarios for systemic risks

criteria.title: Risk identification process
criteria.requirement: Identify systemic risks using a structured process and inputs defined in Appendix 1 (risk types, nature, sources, specified risks).
criteria.assessment_question: Is there a documented systemic risk identification process aligned with Appendix 1 (types, sources, specified risks) and multiple information sources?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Mapping to appendix reference.
Documentation includes Use of post-market/incidents/other sources.
Documentation includes Rationale for included risks.
Indicative keywords/phrases: identify, systemic, risks, using, structured, process, inputs, defined, appendix, risk, types, nature

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: Risk scenarios
criteria.requirement: Develop systemic risk scenarios for each identified systemic risk.
criteria.assessment_question: Are systemic risk scenarios documented for all identified risks with adequate detail and linkage to the risk register?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Scenario documents per risk.
Indicative keywords/phrases: develop, systemic, risk, scenarios, each, identified, scenario, documents, mapping, justification, number, detail

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. commitments.title: Systemic Risk Analysis
commitments.description: Analyze systemic risks through model-independent information, evaluations, modelling, estimation, and monitoring

criteria.title: Model-independent information
criteria.requirement: Gather model-independent information to support systemic risk analysis using methods appropriate in breadth and depth.
criteria.assessment_question: Is model-independent information systematically gathered and used to inform evaluations, modelling, and estimation?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Expert panel or participatory method notes.
Documentation includes Decision trace linking info to evaluation design.
Indicative keywords/phrases: gather, model-independent, information, support, systemic, risk, analysis, using, methods, appropriate, breadth, depth

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Model evaluations
criteria.requirement: Conduct state-of-the-art model evaluations to analyse systemic risks, including open-ended testing and appropriate elicitation and mitigation-effectiveness assessment.
criteria.assessment_question: Are state-of-the-art model evaluations conducted and documented according to Appendix 3 guidance?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes (If applicable) independent external evaluator reports.
Indicative keywords/phrases: conduct, state-of-the-art, model, evaluations, analyse, systemic, risks, open-ended, testing, appropriate, elicitation, mitigation-effectiveness

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Risk modelling
criteria.requirement: Conduct systemic risk modelling using appropriate state-of-the-art methods grounded in scenarios and identification outputs.
criteria.assessment_question: Is systemic risk modelling documented and linked to scenarios and identified risk sources?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Risk model documents (pathways/causal chains).
Documentation includes Linkage to scenarios (measure reference).
Indicative keywords/phrases: conduct, systemic, risk, modelling, using, appropriate, state-of-the-art, methods, grounded, scenarios, identification, outputs

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Risk estimation
criteria.requirement: Estimate probability and severity of systemic risks using appropriate methods and represent results (score/matrix/distribution).
criteria.assessment_question: Are probability and severity estimates documented for each systemic risk with methods and assumptions?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Risk score/matrix/distribution per risk.
Documentation includes Use of evaluation and incident inputs.
Indicative keywords/phrases: estimate, probability, severity, systemic, risks, using, appropriate, methods, represent, results, score, matrix

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Post-market monitoring
criteria.requirement: Conduct post-market monitoring and (where required) provide adequate access to independent external evaluators under appropriate safeguards.
criteria.assessment_question: Is there a post-market monitoring program and, where applicable, an independent external evaluator access process with safeguards?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Evaluator access policy + access logs.
Indicative keywords/phrases: conduct, post-market, monitoring, required, provide, adequate, access, independent, external, evaluators, under, appropriate

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. commitments.title: Risk Acceptance & Decisions
commitments.description: Define acceptance criteria and make proceeding decisions based on risk acceptability

criteria.title: Acceptance criteria
criteria.requirement: Define and apply systemic risk acceptance criteria/tiers with safety margins, and use them to determine acceptability per risk and overall.
criteria.assessment_question: Are acceptance criteria/tiers (measurable) defined per systemic risk and applied with safety margins to determine acceptability?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Acceptance tier definitions per risk.
Documentation includes Decision records applying criteria.
Indicative keywords/phrases: define, apply, systemic, risk, acceptance, criteria, tiers, safety, margins, use, them, determine

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. The requirement can reasonably be considered defined at a documentation level (i.e., sufficiently specified to guide implementation), with no material gaps.

compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: Proceeding decisions
criteria.requirement: Proceed with development/market/use only when systemic risks are acceptable; otherwise restrict/withdraw/recall as needed, implement mitigations, and repeat assessment steps.
criteria.assessment_question: Are go/no-go controls defined and enforced, with defined actions and reassessment loops when risks are unacceptable or likely to become unacceptable?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: proceed, development, market, use, systemic, risks, acceptable, otherwise, restrict, withdraw, recall, needed

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. commitments.title: Safety Mitigations
commitments.description: Implement safety mitigations robust under adversarial pressure

criteria.title: Safety mitigations
criteria.requirement: Implement safety mitigations appropriate to systemic risks and robust under adversarial pressure, aligned with release/distribution strategy.
criteria.assessment_question: Are safety mitigations documented, implemented, and tested for robustness against adversarial pressure?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Mitigation designs and configs.
Documentation includes Access staging policy (if used).
Indicative keywords/phrases: implement, safety, mitigations, appropriate, systemic, risks, robust, under, adversarial, pressure, aligned, release

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. commitments.title: Security Protections
commitments.description: Define security goals and implement appropriate security mitigations

criteria.title: Security goal
criteria.requirement: Define a Security Goal specifying the threat actors the security mitigations protect against, considering current and expected model capabilities.
criteria.assessment_question: Is a Security Goal explicitly defined (including external and insider threat actors) and capability-informed?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Threat actor scope + assumptions.
Indicative keywords/phrases: define, security, goal, specifying, threat, actors, mitigations, protect, against, considering, current, expected

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. The requirement can reasonably be considered defined at a documentation level (i.e., sufficiently specified to guide implementation), with no material gaps.

compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: Security mitigations
criteria.requirement: Implement appropriate security mitigations to meet the Security Goal, including measures aligned with Appendix 4 objectives (or justified alternatives) and staged implementation if needed.
criteria.assessment_question: Are security mitigations implemented and mapped to objectives (Appendix 4) or justified equivalents, with staging documented where relevant?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Security controls inventory mapped to objectives.
Documentation includes Unreleased weights protection (where applicable).
Documentation includes Security assurance evidence (reviews/red-teaming/EDR/IDS etc.).
Indicative keywords/phrases: implement, appropriate, security, mitigations, meet, goal, measures, aligned, appendix, objectives, justified, alternatives

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. The requirement can reasonably be considered defined at a documentation level (i.e., sufficiently specified to guide implementation), with no material gaps.

commitments.title: Model Reporting
commitments.description: Create and maintain comprehensive Model Reports with required documentation

criteria.title: Model description
criteria.requirement: Provide Model Report information describing the model, intended use, version differences, and model specification (including system prompt and refusal topics).
criteria.assessment_question: Does the Model Report include the required model description, intended use, version differences, and specification?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Model Report section on description/specification.
Indicative keywords/phrases: provide, model, report, information, describing, intended, use, version, differences, specification, system, prompt

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Reasons for proceeding
criteria.requirement: Justify proceeding based on acceptability determination (incl. safety margins), define foreseeable undermining conditions, and describe decision-making and external inputs.
criteria.assessment_question: Does the Model Report justify proceeding, list undermining conditions, and describe decision process and inputs?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Acceptability justification + safety margins.
Documentation includes Decision records and external inputs.
Indicative keywords/phrases: justify, proceeding, based, acceptability, determination, incl, safety, margins, define, foreseeable, undermining, conditions

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Risk documentation
criteria.requirement: Document systemic risk identification, analysis, estimation, evaluations, mitigations (safety & security), limitations, and forward-looking plans in the Model Report.
criteria.assessment_question: Is comprehensive documentation of identification/analysis/mitigation present in the Model Report with sufficient detail and traceability?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Security goal + control mapping.
Indicative keywords/phrases: document, systemic, risk, identification, analysis, estimation, evaluations, mitigations, safety, security, limitations, forward-looking

criteria.title: External reports
criteria.requirement: Provide available independent external evaluator reports and independent security review reports (or justify absence) consistent with confidentiality constraints.
criteria.assessment_question: Are external evaluation/security review reports included or the absence justified, and is evaluator selection explained when applicable?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: provide, available, independent, external, evaluator, reports, security, review, justify, absence, consistent, confidentiality

criteria.title: Risk landscape changes
criteria.requirement: Include information enabling understanding of material changes to the systemic risk landscape (e.g., scaling, architectures, training techniques, mitigation effectiveness).
criteria.assessment_question: Does the Model Report describe relevant material changes to the systemic risk landscape and implications?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: information, enabling, understanding, material, changes, systemic, risk, landscape, scaling, architectures, training, techniques

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. The requirement can reasonably be considered defined at a documentation level (i.e., sufficiently specified to guide implementation), with no material gaps.
compliance_rubric.PARTIALLY_COMPLIANT: Some relevant concepts or elements required by the measure are present, but they are incomplete, implicit, vaguely described, not clearly delineated, or only partially mapped to the measure’s expected evidence. As a result, the measure is only partially addressed at a documentation level.
compliance_rubric.NOT_COMPLIANT: No meaningful evidence addressing the requirement is present in the provided documentation, or the documentation contradicts the requirement.
compliance_rubric.NOT_EVIDENCED / UNKNOWN: The provided documentation is insufficient to determine whether the requirement is addressed (e.g., missing sections, inaccessible content, or lack of verifiable references).

criteria.title: Model report updates
criteria.requirement: Update the Model Report when acceptability justification is materially undermined and periodically for most capable models, with versioning and changelogs.
criteria.assessment_question: Are update triggers, timing controls, periodicity rules, and versioned changelogs defined and evidenced?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Update SOP + trigger criteria.
Documentation includes Examples of updates + changelog/version.
Documentation includes Release gating for deliberate changes.
Documentation includes Periodic update schedule evidence.
Indicative keywords/phrases: update, model, report, acceptability, justification, materially, undermined, periodically, most, capable, models, versioning

criteria.title: AI Office notification
criteria.requirement: Provide AI Office access to the Model Report by market placement and provide access to updates within five business days (with interim reporting rules where delays apply).
criteria.assessment_question: Is there evidence of providing AI Office access at market placement and timely access to updates (incl. interim reporting if delayed)?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: provide, office, access, model, report, market, placement, updates, within, five, business, days

commitments.title: Governance & Resources
commitments.description: Establish governance, allocate resources, and promote healthy risk culture

criteria.title: Responsibilities
criteria.requirement: Define and allocate responsibilities for systemic risk management across governance levels (oversight, ownership, support/monitoring, assurance).
criteria.assessment_question: Are responsibilities clearly allocated across governance levels with role holders and independence where needed?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes Committee terms of reference.
Indicative keywords/phrases: define, allocate, responsibilities, systemic, risk, management, across, governance, levels, oversight, ownership, support

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Resources

criteria.title: Risk culture
criteria.requirement: Promote a healthy systemic risk culture (tone from top, challenge mechanisms, reporting channels, whistleblower protections, non-retaliation).

expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: promote, healthy, systemic, risk, culture, tone, top, challenge, mechanisms, reporting, channels, whistleblower

commitments.title: Serious Incident Management
commitments.description: Identify, document, report, and retain serious incident information

criteria.title: Incident identification
criteria.requirement: Use methods and channels to identify and track serious incidents, including monitoring external sources and facilitating third-party reporting.
criteria.assessment_question: Are serious incident identification methods and reporting channels (incl. third-party) documented and operational?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: use, methods, channels, identify, track, serious, incidents, monitoring, external, sources, facilitating, third-party

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Incident documentation
criteria.requirement: Track/document/report serious incidents with the required minimum information set, including root cause analysis and mitigation failures/circumvention.
criteria.assessment_question: Do incident reports capture the required information set and document missing information appropriately?
expected_evidence: Documentation includes RCA procedure (incl. mitigation failure).
Indicative keywords/phrases: track, document, report, serious, incidents, required, minimum, information, set, root, cause, analysis

compliance_rubric.COMPLIANT: Clear and explicit evidence in the provided documentation shows that all required concepts and elements of the measure are addressed in a concrete and unambiguous manner. criteria.title: Reporting timelines
criteria.requirement: Report serious incidents within specified timelines and provide intermediate and final reports as required.
criteria.assessment_question: Are incident reporting timelines defined and adhered to (initial/intermediate/final)?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: report, serious, incidents, within, specified, timelines, provide, intermediate, final, reports, required, timeline

criteria.title: Retention
criteria.requirement: Retain serious-incident documentation for at least five years from the later of documentation date or incident date.
criteria.assessment_question: Is a retention policy implemented that meets or exceeds the 5-year requirement?
expected_evidence: Indicative keywords/phrases: retain, serious-incident, documentation, least, five, years, later, date, incident, retention, policy, archival
